description: Mobile web phase A5 (docs/design/native-app-surfaces.md): Safari on a managed iOS simulator. On a capable macOS host the ios context resolves the newest installed iPhone simulator via simctl, opens one Safari session through the lazily-installed XCUITest driver, and goTo/find/click/screenshot behave exactly as on desktop — element semantics are web DOM. On Windows/Linux the toolchain probe lands the context SKIPPED with actionable macOS/Xcode guidance. The first XCUITest session on a cold runner builds WebDriverAgent via xcodebuild (minutes) — the session's default WDA ceiling accommodates it, and CI caches the build. iOS simulators share the host network, so the job's test server is reachable at localhost:8092 directly.

tests.description: No `browsers` key on the ios entry: the platform default (safari) fills in. Navigate, assert page text, click a button, assert the click's side effect, and capture the device screen.
Explicit `browsers: safari` on the ios entry — on a mobile pair the name stays `safari` (no desktop webkit aliasing) and means the real device Safari.
